sanskrit:
आहारस्त्वपि सर्वस्य त्रिविधो भवति प्रियः।
यज्ञस्तपस्तथा दानं तेषां भेदमिमं श्रृणु॥
transliteration:
āhāras tv api sarvasya tri-vidho bhavati priyaḥ |
yajñas tapas tathā dānaṃ teṣāṃ bhedam imaṃ śṛṇu ||
literal_meaning: Even the food of all beings is of three kinds, dear; sacrifice, austerity, and charity are likewise. Hear now their distinctions.
contextual_meaning: Krishna alerts Arjuna that every human act—including the most basic, like eating—carries a psychological signature (sattva, rajas, tamas). Discernment of these signatures is the first step toward intentional living.
psychological_state: Pre-contemplative sharpening—mind is primed to sort its own behaviors into helpful vs. unhelpful buckets.
trigger_scenario: Standing in a supermarket aisle wondering whether to buy the organic salad or the chips—realizing the choice is not just nutritional but reveals your current mind-state.
anchor_text: Notice the inner flavor behind every bite, gift, or deed.
